trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade relationship. The UK exports financial services, pharmaceuticals, and luxury goods to South Korea. South Korea exports electronics, automobiles, ships, and semiconductors to the UK. The UK-Korea FTA (2019, rolling over EU-Korea FTA) maintains preferential trade terms.

diplomatic.summary: The UK and South Korea signed a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2023. Both countries cooperate closely on Indo-Pacific security, North Korea sanctions enforcement, and multilateral governance. South Korea was invited to G7 summits. UK-Korea alignment on Ukraine support and Russia sanctions is close. Cultural ties growing through Korean Wave popularity in UK.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. South Korea's emergence as a major arms exporter and defence industrial power creates new partnership opportunities. UK and Korean navies participate in joint exercises. Both countries share threat assessments on North Korea and China. UK interest in Korean industrial capacity for European rearmament supply chains.

financial.summary: London is a hub for Korean companies' European financial operations. Major Korean firms (Samsung, Hyundai, LG) have UK operations. Korean EV battery investments in UK (Samsung SDI, etc.) are strategically significant. Korean sovereign funds invest in UK assets.

energy.summary: Some bilateral energy technology cooperation, particularly in nuclear (both countries have strong nuclear industries). UK interest in Korean offshore wind technology. Korean interest in UK's advanced nuclear expertise (Rolls-Royce SMRs).

scientific.summary: Strong academic and research cooperation. Korean students are a large presence in UK universities. Joint research in AI, semiconductors, materials, and life sciences. UK-Korea Science and Technology cooperation agreement active.
